statement: Judge-gym should keep llm_prompt_templates and replace runtime-shaped llm_requests behavior with an append-only Convex LLM attempt ledger keyed by Temporal activity idempotency identifiers.
rationale: Temporal Activities are not exactly once, Temporal history is a poor default store for large LLM payloads, and judge-gym still needs reproducibility plus dedupe across retries.
keyAssumptions: Convex can store metadata and hashes/blob references without becoming the primary payload bottleneck
Activity writes into the ledger are made idempotent with unique idempotency keys
validationCriteria: Duplicate Activity execution does not create duplicate scientific artifacts or duplicate ledger effects
Prompts and outputs remain queryable and reproducible without relying on Temporal history retention
critiquePoints: Blindly storing full prompt and output bodies in Convex could create avoidable cost and throughput pressure
questions: Which payloads stay inline in Convex and which move to blob/object storage?
